summary: Install-time wallet, SSH, AWS, and npm credential stealer exfiltrating to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: Package @wagni_bot/hyperliquid-sdk@1.1.5 impersonates a Hyperliquid exchange SDK but ships no SDK functionality; its package.json sets both preinstall and postinstall to run postinstall.js, and points main at the same script. On install, postinstall.js walks the user's home directory to depth 3 searching for crypto wallet artifacts (id.json, wallet.json, keystore.json, seed.txt, mnemonic.txt,.env* and similar), and reads ~/.ssh keys, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.git-credentials, and ~/.npmrc auth tokens. Each harvested file's contents (up to 10KB) are combined with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, and cwd into a JSON body and POSTed via plain HTTP to http://107.161.90.180:7777. The name impersonates the Hyperliquid SDK to target crypto developers whose machines are more likely to contain wallet keys.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
